title: Horizon Depth vs. Policy Depth

runtime.expected_outcome: Horizon controls the predictive belief chain length; policy_depth controls committed action-sequence length. Recipe makes the distinction visible on one maze.

audiences.kid: How far ahead you imagine and how many moves you commit to are different questions. The first is bigger; the second is shorter.
audiences.real: Horizon is how many steps the agent rolls beliefs forward; policy depth is the commitment length of the action sequence evaluated. They can be tuned independently.
audiences.equation: EFE sums over tau=1..T. The policy space is enumerated up to depth D<=T. Increasing T lets the agent see further; increasing D makes it commit harder.
audiences.derivation: Decomposing G into tau-indexed contributions is a direct consequence of the factorised predictive belief q(s_1, ..., s_T | pi) = prod_tau q(s_tau | pi). Policy depth is a separate choice about the action factorisation.

orchestrate.objective: Separate horizon and policy_depth and show how each independently affects behaviour.
orchestrate.role: Planning-knob tutor disambiguating two commonly-conflated parameters.
orchestrate.context: corridor_turns 5x5; vary (horizon, policy_depth) in {(2,2), (6,2), (6,4)} to isolate each effect.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4 & App B.
